notes: Severity: P0 = demo-blocking or security-critical; P1 = must fix before a serious demo; P2 = quality/reliability/release hygiene; P3 = cleanup/parity backlog. Evidence is file:line at the audited head. No risky rewrites were performed; only the four guardrails in FR-AUDIT-001/004/011 and the gated internal panel were implemented in this pass.

issues.title: Internal codename "TrueLine" leaks into the customer-facing browser title and across the UI
issues.description: The global document title and template still use the internal engine codename, so the very first thing a buyer sees in their browser tab is the wrong brand. The product brand is FieldRoute.
issues.recommended_fix: Set metadata title default to 'FieldRoute — OSP redline & closeout' and template to '%s · FieldRoute'. Sweep all customer-facing surfaces for 'TrueLine'/'TRUELINE'.
issues.test_or_verification: CI grep: no occurrence of /TrueLine/i renders under customer routes; manual: tab title reads FieldRoute.
issues.customer_impact: Buyer sees the wrong product name immediately; reads as unfinished/whitelabel.
issues.competitive_gap: Basic brand polish competitors have by default.

issues.title: No app-level auth; tenant isolation depends on a client-supplied header plus an external access gate
issues.description: Identity is carried by client-set X-TL-Tenant / X-TL-Session headers, documented in-code as a 'dev stand-in, NOT real auth'. There is no application authentication; tenant isolation relies entirely on an external Cloudflare Access gate. If Access is bypassed or misconfigured, a client can set any tenant value and read/write another tenant's project and uploads.
issues.recommended_fix: Add real session auth; derive tenant server-side from the authenticated session; treat X-TL-Tenant as untrusted until the backend enforces scope. Document the trust boundary. Add per-tenant authorization checks on every /v2/product route.
issues.test_or_verification: Integration test: a request with tenant B's header cannot read tenant A's job; negative test returns 403.
issues.customer_impact: Cross-tenant data exposure if the external gate fails; a hard blocker for any buyer security review.
issues.competitive_gap: Single-source-of-truth with role/tenant isolation is table stakes for multi-party construction tools.

issues.title: Customer-reachable /showcase leaks engine env-var names and jargon in its not-configured/error states
issues.description: /showcase is linked from the homepage entry cards and is NOT redirected by middleware, so a customer can open it. When product mode is off or a read fails, it renders raw env var names (NEXT_PUBLIC_TL2_PRODUCT_API / _API_BASE / _TENANT) and engine terms like 'frontier' to the user.
issues.recommended_fix: Replace the env-var/jargon error copy with a neutral 'Finished examples are temporarily unavailable.' Keep raw config hints behind the internal flag only.
issues.test_or_verification: Render /showcase with product mode off; assert no 'NEXT_PUBLIC_', 'TL2', or 'frontier' string in output.
issues.customer_impact: Exposes internal configuration and jargon on a page we explicitly invite customers to open.
issues.competitive_gap: Polished empty/error states are expected baseline.

issues.title: Unbounded base64-JSON uploads with no size cap
issues.description: Files are read fully into memory, base64-encoded, and POSTed as a JSON field with no client or evidenced server size limit. A large PDF/photo can OOM the browser tab and produces an unbounded request body. base64 inflates payloads ~33%.
issues.recommended_fix: Enforce a client-side per-file size cap before encoding (done in this pass) AND a server-side cap; move large uploads to streamed/multipart instead of base64-JSON.
issues.test_or_verification: Select a file above the cap; assert it is rejected with a plain-English message and no POST is made.
issues.customer_impact: Crashed tab / failed uploads on real plan sets (often large); poor field reliability.
issues.competitive_gap: Robust large-file upload is expected for plan PDFs.

issues.title: HECTOR_DEMO_CHECKLIST.md ships a person name, a local Windows path, and repo-state notes
issues.description: A repo doc named after a person contains a named individual, a personal Windows path (C:\Nova\projects\...), 'no configured Git remote' notes, and backup filenames. Embarrassing and against the no-hardcoded-names rule.
issues.recommended_fix: Delete the file or replace with a generic, name-free 'demo_checklist.md' that references generic projects only.
issues.test_or_verification: Repo grep for the person name and 'C:\\Nova' returns nothing.
issues.customer_impact: If shared or leaked, exposes internal names/paths and an unfinished posture.

issues.title: Entire legacy mock app still present, hidden only by a middleware redirect
issues.description: The original V1/V2 surfaces remain in the repo with customer-embarrassing content: 'Demo Project 001 — v2 staging', 'Mock UI demo queue — not engine data', mock decision buttons, disabled comment box. They are reachable only by URL because middleware redirects them, so a single config change or unmatched path exposes them; they also bloat the bundle and attack surface.
issues.recommended_fix: Delete src/app/{map,plans,redlines,closeout,evidence,feed,packet,settings,projects} and the mock tree once /showcase + /intake are confirmed to cover the demo; remove the now-unneeded redirect list.
issues.test_or_verification: Routes return 404; CI grep finds no 'Demo Project'/'Sample Fiber'/'mock decision' in src/app.
issues.customer_impact: One click/edit from showing a fake dev console to a contractor.

issues.title: /settings page: hardcoded org/region/persons/emails, disabled-button traps, codename
issues.description: Settings renders a fake org ('TrueLine Preview Org'), a hardcoded region ('Central Texas'), four invented members with @truelinepreview.com emails, disabled 'Connect engine'/'Configure' buttons, and 'TrueLine v2 engine'.
issues.recommended_fix: Delete the page with the rest of the legacy app; if a settings surface is needed later, render only real, session-derived data with no disabled placeholders.
issues.test_or_verification: Route 404s; grep finds no fabricated person/email under src/app.
issues.customer_impact: Reads as fake/unfinished; leaks codename and a fictional org/region.

issues.title: README and PRODUCT_UX_PLAN describe redirected surfaces as the product; brand is TrueLine throughout docs
issues.description: Top-level docs present Dashboard/Hero Map/Plan Viewer/Redline Review/etc. as the product and brand it 'TrueLine', contradicting the shipped reality (those routes redirect to /intake) and the FieldRoute brand.
issues.recommended_fix: Rewrite README + UX plan to FieldRoute and the /intake workspace; mark legacy surfaces as deprecated/removed. Point future sessions to docs/product/fieldroute_product_direction.md.
issues.test_or_verification: Docs reviewed; brand grep clean; described routes match shipped routes.
issues.customer_impact: Misleads anyone (incl. future engineers) about what the product is.

issues.title: Engine jargon leaks into live customer-facing copy in the /intake workspace
issues.description: Customer-visible strings expose internal terms: 'source anchor', 'corpus', 'frontier', 'reviewed bore-log', 'engine_ready', and raw extraction codes MANUAL_ENTRY/TABLE_IMPORT. An OSP/HDD operator reads these as developer jargon.
issues.recommended_fix: Translate to plain English ('mark the route', 'recognized project', 'progress 50/58', 'confirmed bore rows'); keep raw codes behind Diagnostics only.
issues.test_or_verification: Grep customer components for the banned term list; assert none render outside a Diagnostics/details block.
issues.customer_impact: Workflow feels like a dev tool, not a field product; raises the explanation burden.
issues.competitive_gap: Plain-language field UX is a baseline expectation.

issues.title: File validation is extension-only; no content/magic-byte check
issues.description: inferUploadKind decides the upload kind purely from the filename extension; a mislabeled or malicious file (e.g. an executable renamed .pdf) is accepted and stored untrusted.
issues.recommended_fix: Validate by magic bytes / sniffed MIME on the server in addition to extension; reject mismatches; keep storing untrusted but never trust the declared type.
issues.test_or_verification: Upload a .pdf with non-PDF magic bytes; assert server rejects it.
issues.customer_impact: Bad data silently enters the pipeline; potential security exposure.
issues.competitive_gap: Content validation is standard for document-ingest products.

issues.title: Sidebar/topbar show a hardcoded status that may not reflect reality
issues.description: The sidebar footer always shows a green 'Live product API' dot and the topbar always claims 'Access-gated', regardless of whether product mode is actually enabled or the API is reachable.
issues.recommended_fix: Drive the footer label/dot from productApiEnabled(); show 'Mock preview data' when product mode is off. Remove the unconditional 'Access-gated' claim or tie it to a real signal.
issues.test_or_verification: With NEXT_PUBLIC_TL2_PRODUCT_API unset, the footer reads a mock/preview state, not 'Live product API'.
issues.customer_impact: Dishonest status undermines trust in every other honest signal in the app.

issues.title: npm run lint fails: 7 react-hooks/set-state-in-effect errors
issues.description: Seven components synchronously call setState within an effect body, which the project's lint config flags as errors, so the lint gate is red.
issues.recommended_fix: Refactor each effect to set state inside an async callback/then, or restructure so the initial state is computed without an effect; then re-enable the rule as an error gate.
issues.test_or_verification: npm run lint exits 0.
issues.customer_impact: Indirect: a red gate hides real regressions and blocks clean CI.

issues.title: contracts:check hardcodes a sibling-repo absolute path and fails off the author's machine
issues.description: The contract-parity check reads /home/user/trueline-field-mobile/src/contracts/index.ts, which does not exist in this environment, so npm run contracts:check throws ENOENT.
issues.recommended_fix: Make the mirror path configurable via env/arg with a clean skip-with-warning when absent; or vendor the mirror into the repo.
issues.test_or_verification: contracts:check passes or cleanly skips in a fresh clone.
issues.customer_impact: n/a; breaks CI/dev reproducibility.

issues.title: Plan sheets stamp 'TRUELINE PREVIEW' on the rendered sheet furniture
issues.description: The plan-sheet title block renders the codename as a watermark/stamp. Dead surface today (redirected), but a brand leak the moment any sheet rendering is reused.
issues.recommended_fix: Remove the stamp or replace with a neutral FieldRoute mark; delete with the legacy plans surface.
issues.test_or_verification: No 'TRUELINE' string in any rendered sheet.
issues.customer_impact: Codename printed on a plan deliverable.

issues.title: next.config hardcodes a staging origin and forces global no-store caching
issues.description: allowedDevOrigins pins 'staging.fieldroute.io' and a headers() rule sets Cache-Control: no-store on every response. Staging-specific config is committed to source; the global no-store will degrade a real production deploy's performance.
issues.recommended_fix: Drive allowedDevOrigins and the cache policy from environment; apply no-store only in staging/preview, not production.
issues.test_or_verification: Production build serves cacheable assets; staging keeps no-store.
issues.customer_impact: Slower production loads; env coupling in source.

issues.title: Disabled-button UX lacks inline rationale in capture/bore-log gates
issues.description: Several primary actions disable on multiple hidden conditions with no inline explanation of why, so the user is stuck without guidance (only the point-count case has a hint).
issues.recommended_fix: Add an inline 'why disabled' note next to each disabled control; these are dev-fallback surfaces but should still be self-explanatory.
issues.test_or_verification: Each disabled state shows a reason string in the DOM.
issues.customer_impact: Dead-end interactions in fallback tooling.

issues.title: Map has no basemap / street context / marker navigation
issues.description: ProductRouteMap draws real route geometry but on a blank canvas with no streets, labels, or markers, and street names are deliberately not shown. A field user cannot orient the route to real streets or navigate to a location.
issues.recommended_fix: Add an owner-approved tiled basemap behind a flag and place markers from our own KMZ features; keep route-only as the decluttered default.
issues.test_or_verification: With the flag on, a basemap renders under the route; markers are clickable; default view unchanged.
issues.customer_impact: Reduced situational awareness in the field.
issues.competitive_gap: Marker/location navigation and area visualization are standard public capabilities.

issues.title: Mock fixtures littered with Sample/demo names
issues.description: The mock data tree contains 'Sample Project A — demo', 'Sample Fiber Co-op (demo)', 'SAMPLE ROAD/LANE/DRIVE', 'DRY CREEK', sample stations, and dated playback events. Only reachable via the legacy surfaces, but a standing landmine while those ship.
issues.recommended_fix: Delete the mock tree with the legacy surfaces; if kept for offline dev, gate it and keep names obviously synthetic and generic.
issues.test_or_verification: No mock fixture is imported by a customer-reachable route.
issues.customer_impact: Only if a legacy surface is exposed.

issues.title: Duplicate map paradigms: mock HeroMapView/MapCanvas vs real ProductRouteMap
issues.description: Two parallel map implementations exist; the mock SVG basemap (HeroMapView/MapCanvas) is dead in the product but maintained, duplicating concepts with the real ProductRouteMap.
issues.recommended_fix: Delete the mock map components with the legacy /map surface; keep ProductRouteMap as the single map.
issues.test_or_verification: Only ProductRouteMap remains; build/tsc green.
issues.customer_impact: n/a (maintenance cost).

issues.title: Field timezone hardcoded to America/Chicago
issues.description: Date formatting assumes a single US Central timezone, baking a regional assumption into a tool meant for multiple markets.
issues.recommended_fix: Derive timezone from project/tenant settings or render in the source timestamp's offset; default sensibly without hardcoding one region.
issues.test_or_verification: A project in another timezone renders correct local times.
issues.customer_impact: Wrong displayed times outside US Central.

issues.title: No field-capture / offline / mobile path on web (parity gap)
issues.description: Web is intentionally office-only, but there is no in-product path or stub toward field photo/GPS/note capture and offline tolerance — a real public-capability parity gap to plan for via a separate field surface on our own contracts.
issues.recommended_fix: Plan a separate field-capture surface on the existing contracts; do not bolt field capture onto the office workspace.
issues.test_or_verification: Roadmap item exists; no field-only workflow added to web without approval.
issues.customer_impact: No field capture today (expected for the office product).
issues.competitive_gap: Mobile capture, live progress, and offline tolerance are standard public field-app capabilities.
